inclusions.description: Unwrought aluminium. Subheading Note 1(a): 'Aluminium, not alloyed' = metal ≥99% Al by weight with each other element below specified limits (e.g., Cu ≤0.1% normally, or ≤0.2% if Cr and Mn each ≤0.05%). Subheading Note 1(b): 'Aluminium alloys' = metallic substances where Al predominates over each other element by weight AND either (i) at least one element or iron+silicon combined exceeds specified limit; or (ii) total other elements >1% by weight. Common alloys: Al-Si, Al-Cu, Al-Mg, Al-Mn, Al-Zn series
Aluminium powders and flakes: (a) powders of non-lamellar structure; (b) powders of lamellar structure (flakes) and flakes
Aluminium plates, sheets and strip, of a thickness exceeding 0.2 mm
Aluminium foil (whether or not printed, or backed with paper, paperboard, plastics or similar backing materials), of a thickness (excluding any backing) not exceeding 0.2 mm
Aluminium tube or pipe fittings (e.g., couplings, elbows, sleeves)

Aluminium structures (excluding prefabricated buildings of heading 94.06) and parts of structures (e.g., bridges and bridge-sections, towers, lattice masts, roofs, roofing frameworks, doors and windows and their frames and thresholds and balustrades, pillars and columns); aluminium plates, rods, profiles, tubes and the like, prepared for use in structures

Aluminium reservoirs, tanks, vats and similar containers, for any material (other than compressed or liquefied gas), of a capacity exceeding 300 litres, whether or not lined or heat-insulated, but not fitted with mechanical or thermal equipment

Aluminium casks, drums, cans, boxes and similar containers (including rigid or collapsible tubular containers), for any material (other than compressed or liquefied gas), of a capacity not exceeding 300 litres, whether or not lined or heat-insulated, but not fitted with mechanical or thermal equipment
Aluminium containers for compressed or liquefied gas
Stranded wire, cables, plaited bands and the like, of aluminium, not electrically insulated

Table, kitchen or other household articles and parts thereof, of aluminium; pot scourers and scouring or polishing pads, gloves and the like, of aluminium; sanitary ware and parts thereof, of aluminium

Other articles of aluminium: nails, tacks, staples (other than those of heading 83.05), screws, bolts, nuts, screw hooks, rivets, cotters, cotter-pins, washers and similar articles; cloth, grill, netting, fencing, reinforcing fabric and similar materials; expanded metal; other articles. Subheading Note 2 (7616.91): 'wire' applies only to products (coils or not) of any cross-sectional shape where NO cross-sectional dimension exceeds 6 mm

exclusions.condition: Aluminium compounds (aluminium oxide/corundum, aluminium chloride, aluminium sulphate, aluminium hydroxide, etc.)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 28 (inorganic aluminium compounds) — e.g., Al2O3 → 28.18; AlCl3 → 28.27. Metallic aluminium → Ch.76; chemical compounds → Ch.28

exclusions.condition: Aluminium-coated base metals (aluminised steel, aluminised wire)
exclusions.action: Redirect to the base metal substrate chapter. Aluminised steel sheet → Ch.72 (72.10). The aluminium coating is incidental; classify by the substrate metal

exclusions.condition: Electrically insulated aluminium wire or cable
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.44 (insulated wire, cable and other insulated electric conductors). Bare uninsulated aluminium wire → 76.05 or 76.14. Iinsulated → 85.44

Redirect to heading 94.06 — Note to 76.10 explicitly excludes prefabricated buildings. Aluminium structural sections/profiles for building construction → 76.10. Complete prefabricated aluminium building → 94.06

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 1 — Pure aluminium vs aluminium alloy: purity threshold and element limits
classification_rules.description: 'Aluminium, not alloyed': ≥99% Al by weight with each individual element below specified table limits. Special rule for copper: Cu up to 0.2% is permitted if Cr ≤0.05% AND Mn ≤0.05%; otherwise Cu limit is 0.1%. 'Aluminium alloys': Al predominates over each other element AND either: (i) at least one element (or Fe+Si combined) exceeds its table limit; or (ii) total other elements >1% by weight. These definitions determine subheading classification within 76.01 (unwrought) and are the basis for classifying alloy vs. non-alloy forms throughout Ch.76.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 2 — 'Wire' for subheading 7616.91: max cross-sectional dimension ≤6 mm
classification_rules.description: For subheading 7616.91 only, 'wire' means products of any cross-sectional shape where NO cross-sectional dimension exceeds 6 mm — whether or not in coils. This overrides the general Section XV Note 9(c) wire definition for this specific subheading. Products with any cross-sectional dimension >6 mm → classified as bars/rods/profiles in 76.04, not as wire in 7616.91.

Thickness of 0.2 mm is the dividing line for flat aluminium products: >0.2 mm → heading 76.06 (plates, sheets and strip). ≤0.2 mm → heading 76.07 (foil). The backing material on foil is excluded from the thickness measurement — only the aluminium thickness determines foil vs. sheet. Aluminium foil may be printed, lacquered or backed with paper/plastics without changing the classification.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 76.04 (bars/rods/profiles) vs 76.05 (wire) — cross-sectional dimension test
classification_rules.description: Per Section XV Note 9(b): 'Wire' = products of cross-sections that would be enclosed in a circle with diameter <13 mm (for purposes of Ch.76, also see Subheading Note 2 for 7616.91). Bars, rods and profiles have larger cross-sections. Extruded aluminium sections (T-profiles, L-profiles, hollow sections) → 76.04. Thin wire for electrical use, bare → 76.05. Stranded wire/cable (multiple wires twisted) → 76.14.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 76.10 (aluminium structures) vs 76.04 (profiles) vs 94.06 (prefabricated buildings)
classification_rules.description: 76.04 = aluminium profiles as raw intermediate products (extruded sections not yet assembled). 76.10 = aluminium profiles or other products PREPARED FOR USE IN STRUCTURES (cut to length, drilled, punched, connected) — door/window frames, bridge sections, lattice masts. 94.06 = complete PREFABRICATED BUILDINGS of aluminium (entire building unit). The degree of completion determines: raw profile → 76.04; building component prepared for assembly → 76.10; complete prefab structure → 94.06.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 76.11 (containers >300L) vs 76.12 (containers ≤300L) vs 76.13 (compressed/liquefied gas containers)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for aluminium containers split by CAPACITY and CONTENT: 76.11 = tanks/vats/reservoirs for any material EXCEPT compressed/liquefied gas, capacity >300 litres. 76.12 = casks/drums/cans/boxes for any material EXCEPT compressed/liquefied gas, capacity ≤300 litres. 76.13 = containers for COMPRESSED or LIQUEFIED GAS only — capacity is irrelevant. NOT fitted with mechanical or thermal equipment (those → Ch.84). Aluminium beer can → 76.12. Aluminium water tower → 76.11. Aluminium gas cylinder → 76.13.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 76.15 (household articles) vs 76.16 (other articles) vs Ch.82/83/84 (functional tools/equipment)
classification_rules.description: 76.15 = aluminium table/kitchen/household articles and sanitary ware (pots, pans, trays, colanders, kitchen utensils). 76.16 = all other aluminium articles including fasteners (nails, screws, rivets), mesh/netting, expanded metal. When an aluminium article corresponds to a specific description in Ch.82 (tools), Ch.83 (miscellaneous metal articles), or Ch.84+ (machinery), those functional chapters take priority over 76.15/76.16. Aluminium padlock → 83.01. Aluminium pressure cooker → 76.15. Aluminium hand tool → Ch.82.
